channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: The Modular Monolith 2026 Complete Guide — Spring Modulith, ArchUnit Fitness Functions, and Lessons from Shopify's 30TB/min Architecture
item.description: The biggest architectural inflection point of 2026 is the "microservices regression" phenomenon. According to the CNCF Q1 2026 report, 42% of organizations that initially adopted microservices...

item.title: The $5 Postcard That "Sank" a $585 Million Warship: How a Cheap Bluetooth Tracker Exposed a Naval Superpower
item.description: The $5 Postcard That "Sank" a $585 Million Warship: How a Cheap Bluetooth Tracker Exposed a Naval Superpower Imagine spending $585 million on a masterpiece of naval engineering—a ship coated in...

item.title: Offline Hash Cracking Tutorial: Crack the Hash Room Walkthrough | TryHackMe
item.description: Now, We will jump to the 2nd level of this -> Question-1: Hash: F09EDCB1FCEFC6DFB23DC3505A882655FF77375ED8AA2D1C13F640FCCC2D0C85 Now, lets crack this with SHA-256 mode 1400 by hashcat Question-2:...

item.title: Change tracking and soft delete: audit trails without the boilerplate
item.description: In most business applications, you need to answer questions like: Who created this record? When was it last modified? Can we undo this deletion? Implementing this by hand means adding columns,...

item.title: Tu WebView no necesita un servidor HTTP. Necesita un Virtual Host.
item.description: Cómo un patrón de los años 90 resolvió uno de los problemas más frustrantes del desarrollo móvil moderno Hay una idea en ingeniería de software que reaparece una y otra vez, con décadas de...

item.title: How to Containerize Your Python App and Deploy to VPS
item.description: Are you tired of the "it works on my machine" problem when deploying your Python applications? Containerization offers a solution by packaging your application and its dependencies into a portable...

item.title: Idempotency in CQRS and Event Sourcing — Part 2: commands, projections and outbox
item.description: In HTTP, idempotency is straightforward: store the key, return the cache. In a CQRS/Event Sourcing system, it's more subtle. The command may be idempotent, but what about the event it generates?...

item.title: Built tasuki — an AI CLI Orchestrator that Seamlessly Hands Off Between Tools
item.description: I built and open-sourced tasuki, an AI CLI orchestrator that automatically rotates between Claude Code / Codex CLI / GitHub Copilot CLI based on priority. Repository:...

item.title: OpenClaw is old? Run Hermes Agent in VS Code through ACP (Agent Client Protocol) now!
item.description: In recent weeks, the Hermes Agent becomes much populor in Agent world. Several users asked me about whether my VS Code ACP Client extension could support Hermes Agent? So in this weekend, I have...

item.title: How I Got OpenStack Keystone Running on Windows: A Collection of Hacks
item.description: Introduction Some projects are born from a simple question: "Will it work?" This is one of them. OpenStack Keystone — the OpenStack cloud identity and authentication service — was designed,...

item.title: Tesla brings its robotaxi service to Dallas and Houston
item.description: The company now offers robotaxi service in three cities, all of them in Texas, after launching in Austin last year and starting to offer rides without safety drivers in January 2026.

item.title: The RAM shortage could last years
item.description: According to Nikkei Asia, even as suppliers ramp up DRAM production, manufacturers are only expected to meet 60 percent of demand by the end of 2027. SK Group chairman has even said that shortages...

item.title: AI chip startup Cerebras files for IPO
item.description: In recent months, the company announced an agreement with Amazon Web Services to use Cerebras chips in Amazon data centers, as well as a deal with OpenAI reportedly worth more than $10 billion.

item.title: Judge rules Trump administration violated the First Amendment in fight against ICE-tracking
item.description: Jorge L. Alonso, a federal district court judge for the Northern District of Illinois, said that the Trump Administration violated the First Amendment when it pressured Facebook and Apple to...

item.title: Cheap stuff that doesn’t suck, take 3
item.description: From fancy OLED TVs to robot vacuums with arms, we tend to cover a lot of cool stuff here at The Verge that, unfortunately, often costs as much as a month’s rent (or more). But with the ongoing...

item.title: Anthropic’s relationship with the Trump administration seems to be thawing
item.description: Despite recently being designated a supply-chain risk by the Pentagon, Anthropic is still talking to high-level members of the Trump administration.

item.title: The App Store is booming again, and AI may be why
item.description: New data from Appfigures shows a swell of new app launches in 2026, suggesting AI tools could be fueling a mobile software boom.

item.title: Dyson’s handheld fan is more powerful and louder than I expected
item.description: Two years ago I attended a picturesque outdoor wedding in August where the hot and humid weather had guests occasionally ducking into their cars to enjoy a blast of AC. Dyson’s new $99.99 handheld...

item.title: There’s nothing like an RPG over vacation
item.description: With a vacation comes a big choice: What game should I focus on during the trip? I thought about grinding out the harder levels of Super Meat Boy 3D, but I was looking for something more chill. I...

item.title: The AI apps are coming for your PC
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 124, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, send me your Coachella fits, and also you can read all the old...

item.title: It Takes 2 Minutes to Hack the EU’s New Age-Verification App
item.description: Plus: Major data breaches at a gym chain and hotel giant, a disruptive DDoS attack against Bluesky, dubious ICE hires, and more.

item.title: Schematik Is ‘Cursor for Hardware.’ Anthropic Wants In
item.description: Schematik is a program that aims to help people vibe code for physical devices. Hopefully, it won’t blow anything up.

item.title: [Webinar] Eliminate Ghost Identities Before They Expose Your Enterprise Data
item.description: In 2024, compromised service accounts and forgotten API keys were behind 68% of cloud breaches. Not phishing. Not weak passwords. Unmanaged non-human identities that nobody was watching. For every...

item.title: $13.74M Hack Shuts Down Sanctioned Grinex Exchange After Intelligence Claims
item.description: Grinex, a Kyrgyzstan-incorporated cryptocurrency exchange sanctioned by the U.K. and the U.S. last year, said it's suspending operations after it blamed Western intelligence agencies for a $13.74...

item.title: Mirai Variant Nexcorium Exploits CVE-2024-3721 to Hijack TBK DVRs for DDoS Botnet
item.description: Threat actors are exploiting security flaws in TBK DVR and end‑of‑life (EoL) TP-Link Wi-Fi routers to deploy Mirai-botnet variants on compromised devices, according to findings from Fortinet...

item.title: Sam Altman’s project World looks to scale its human verification empire. First stop: Tinder.
item.description: World, which has raised eyebrows (but also a lot of interest) with its Orb-centered anonymous verification project, is looking to expand its influence via a bevy of new partnerships.

item.title: US-sanctioned currency exchange says $15 million heist done by "unfriendly states"
item.description: Grinex says needed hacking resources "available exclusively to ... unfriendly states."

item.title: OpenAI’s former Sora boss is leaving
item.description: Last month, OpenAI gave up on its Sora video generation tool, and on Friday, the Sora team's leader, Bill Peebles, announced that he is leaving the company. OpenAI has been shifting its priorities...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: New Giant Squid Video
item.description: Pretty fantastic video from Japan of a giant squid eating another squid. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t covered. Blog...

item.title: Should you stare into Sam Altman’s orb before your next date?
item.description: Tinder users who prove they're a real person by visiting an identity-verifying orb will soon be able to get five free boosts in the app - and it's just the latest service to embrace the orb....
